channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ohio Supreme Court hears arguments in watchdog group's records dispute with state attorney general
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The Ohio Supreme Court heard oral arguments Wednesday in a long-running public records case pitting the state’s top law enforcement officer against a national watchdog group that is digging into his ties with the Republican Attorneys General Association.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Photos capture wildfires ravaging Southern California
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: NY top court denies delaying Trump sentencing
item.description: New York’s high court on Thursday declined to halt President-elect Trump’s sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction, which is scheduled for Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: New York's highest appeals court declines to block Trump's sentencing in hush money case
item.description: New York’s highest court has declined to block Donald Trump’s upcoming sentencing in his hush money case, leaving the U.S. Supreme Court as the president-elect’s likely last option to prevent the hearing from taking place Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lebanon's parliament elects army commander Joseph Aoun as president, ending a 2-year deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s parliament voted Thursday to elect army commander Joseph Aoun as head of state, filling a more than two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Schools cancel classes across the Southern US as another burst of winter storms move in
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Another blast of winter storms in the U.S. on Thursday closed schools, snarled flights and put millions of residents across the South under warnings of snow and freezing rain that could cause dangerous travel conditions.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LIVE: Thousands of homes burn in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: More than 100,000 people are under evacuation orders, and the fires have consumed about 45 square miles — roughly the size of the city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fire hydrants ran dry in Southern California just when they were needed most
item.description: The water system used to fight the Palisades fire in Los Angeles buckled under the demands of what turned out to be the most destructive fire in city history, with some hydrants running dry as they were overstressed without assistance from firefighting aircraft for hours early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Biden celebrates great-grandchild, takes no questions at California wildfire briefing with Newsom
item.description: President Biden made some remarks celebrating his great-grandchild while in California following a wildfire briefing. Neither he nor Gov. Gavin Newsom took media questions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jingle! contract workers say they're still waiting to get paid
item.description: Joe and Nikol Straws, two popular DJs from Wyandotte County, performed at Jingle! in Kansas City, Kansas, and said they're owed $18,600.
item.dc:creator: Sean McDowell, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio's COVID-era health director becomes first Dem to file for governor
item.description: “I’m a doctor, not a politician,” she said in a statement announcing her candidacy. “I solve problems by bringing people together regardless of party to find solutions.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus holds its fire on Johnson’s strategy for passing Trump agenda
item.description: Members of the conservative House Freedom Caucus are holding their fire despite Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) bucking their preferred strategy for passing President-elect Trump’s agenda, arguing that the ultimate product is more important than the configuration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden casts doubt on his fitness to serve another four years days before term ends
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden, in a new interview days before he leaves office, cast doubt on his fitness to serve another four years even as he maintained that he could have won election to a second term.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, ABC 4

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KRON4

item.title: Man accused of setting woman on fire on Brooklyn subway pleads not guilty
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta has been accused of intentionally setting fire to 57-year-old Debrina Kawam of New Jersey at an NYC subway station on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden creating two national monuments in California
item.description: President Biden is creating two new national monuments in California, the White House announced Tuesday. Biden will officially designate the Chuckwalla National Monument and the Sáttítla Highlands National Monument — protecting a combined 848,000 acres of particular significance to tribes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's inauguration coincides with an unprecedented string of high-stakes security events in DC
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The last time a president was inaugurated, there was a global pandemic and a violent siege at the very spot at the U.S. Capitol where the ceremony takes place.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Harris calls it a 'good day' for democracy as she oversees peaceful certification of Trump's victory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was no drama on Monday as Kamala Harris oversaw the ceremonial certification of her defeat to Donald Trump. The vice president kept her remarks perfunctory, and she stood with her hands clasped in front of her while the results from each state were read out loud.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Court proceedings for man charged in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing are delayed several weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a New York City hotel is unlikely to appear in Manhattan federal court again before mid-February after prosecutors and lawyers on Monday requested more time to prepare the case for trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, ABC 4

item.title: After 2 years of shortfalls, California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit
item.description: TURLOCK, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Self-help author James Arthur Ray, whose seminar led to tragedy and death, dies at 67
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — James Arthur Ray, a self-help guru whose multimillion-dollar business toppled after he led a sweat lodge ceremony in Arizona that left three people dead, has died. He was 67.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9
